prevalence.trend: stable; increased detection with mental health awareness

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to exposure therapy
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: obsessions and/or compulsions that are time-consuming or cause impairment; differentiate from psychosis and tics

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often diagnosed in psychiatric services; APS recognition variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SSRIs (often higher doses) + CBT with exposure and response prevention (ERP); augmentation strategies in specialist care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited availability of ERP-trained therapists and long wait times for specialized psychotherapy
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of SSRIs via SUS and growing CBT capacity in some centers
